title: Per-source licence verification + provenance record for the chosen indication's sources (M2 licence gate)
context: Before assembling research cards for the chosen target panel (010), every data source feeding that indication must have its reuse terms verified and recorded per release. This is a hard prerequisite for M2 card production: no source may feed a card unless its licence has been verified by a compliance reviewer. Share-alike sources (ChEMBL, HPA, IUPHAR) must be flagged for the governance decision before their data is copied. COSMIC and OncoKB must be confirmed excluded from card output. This depends on the panel decision (003) and the source adapters (006).
objective: Verify and record reuse terms per source release for every data source feeding the chosen cancer indication: licence name, licenseClass (open/open-sa/non-commercial/controlled-access-excluded), attribution string, version, URL, and retrieval date for each. Confirm COSMIC and OncoKB are excluded from card output (pointer-only). Flag all CC-BY-SA sources (ChEMBL, HPA, IUPHAR) for the share-alike governance decision before their data is copied into output. Record all decisions with compliance-reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Reuse terms verified and recorded per source release for every source feeding the chosen indication: licence name, licenseClass, attribution string, version, URL, and retrieval date committed to the licence registry
COSMIC and OncoKB confirmed excluded from card output (pointer-only under their own terms); no COSMIC or OncoKB data values appear in any card's assertions

All CC-BY-SA sources (ChEMBL, HPA, IUPHAR/BPS) are identified, flagged as open-sa class, and held pending the dataset-licence governance decision (CC-BY vs CC-BY-SA); no open-sa source data is copied into card output until the governance decision is recorded
Transitive share-alike attribution is verified: Open Targets entries sourced from ChEMBL carry the open-sa class in upstreamProvenance[] even though Open Targets itself is CC0
Compliance reviewer reviews and signs off on the per-source licence record before M2 card production (010) begins; sign-off committed to the repository
Any source feeding the indication that is not in the compliance gate registry is flagged as a gate violation
output: A committed per-source licence record for every source feeding the chosen indication (licence, licenseClass, attribution string, version, URL, retrieval date), with compliance-reviewer sign-off, confirmed COSMIC/OncoKB exclusion, and open-sa sources flagged pending the governance decision — the M2 licence gate that unblocks card assembly (010).
